question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests), causing the retry logic at line 91 to set retry=true and loop through all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries attempts, each time receiving 429, consuming the full retry budget and then failing, preventing the pinger from ever building a node map and stopping all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 1000000), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to loop for an extremely long time if the mirror endpoint returns 429 or 5xx, blocking the pinger startup indefinitely and preventing any transaction submissions to consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted value into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST that includes path traversal sequences (e.g., 'http://mirror-rest:5551/../../../etc/passwd'), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to construct a URL that, when fetched, accesses unintended internal endpoints and leaks sensitive data that could be used to further compromise node infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an internal network address (e.g., 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/') to perform SSRF, causing the pinger to fetch cloud metadata and expose credentials that could be used to reconfigure or shut down >=30% of network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to 'http://10.0.0.1/api/v1' (an internal Kubernetes service address), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make SSRF requests to internal cluster services, potentially fetching node configuration data that reveals which nodes can be targeted to achieve >=30% shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing IPv6 addresses or hostnames that resolve to loopback (127.0.0.1), causing the pinger's SDK client to attempt gRPC connections to localhost, failing all transaction submissions and causing all nodes to lose pinger-driven health signals?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the publicly visible default key at line 40 ('302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137'), and use that key outside the pinger to submit a flood of transactions directly to specific consensus nodes, causing >=30% of them to be overwhelmed by signature verification load?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a value that, after toDuration() parsing, produces exactly 0 (e.g., '0s'), bypassing the interval <= 0 check at line 114 only if the check is strict less-than-or-equal, causing time.NewTicker(0) in main() to panic and crash the pinger process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a duration string with an extremely large numeric component (e.g., '9223372036854775807ns') to HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL, causing toDuration() to return a near-maximum time.Duration that makes the ticker fire only once every ~292 years, effectively stopping all transaction submissions to all consensus nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that returns a valid nodesEnvelope listing only 1 out of 7 consensus nodes, causing the SDK client to route all transactions to that single node, while the remaining 6 nodes (>85%) receive no pinger traffic and their liveness probes eventually fail?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint serve a response that is valid JSON but contains a 'nodes' array with all entries having node_account_id set to '', causing all entries to be skipped at line 103, resulting in an empty network map and a startup failure that prevents all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a response with a 'links.next' field pointing to an attacker-controlled URL, and if the pinger follows pagination links, cause it to fetch an infinite chain of pages that exhausts memory and CPU, crashing the pinger and halting all node transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '30s' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 30, causing backoff() to compute exponentially growing sleep durations that quickly hit the 30s cap, making the pinger spend up to 30*30s=900s per failed tick in retry sleep, effectively stopping transaction submissions to all nodes for extended periods?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a newline character into HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST (e.g., 'http://mirror-rest:5551\nX-Injected: header'), causing HTTP header injection in the request built by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork(), potentially bypassing authentication or routing the request to an unintended backend that returns a poisoned node list?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '1ns', causing every HTTP request in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to time out immediately, making buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() exhaust all retries and fail, preventing the pinger from ever building a node map and stopping all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint serve a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoints contain domain names that resolve to the IP addresses of only a specific subset of consensus nodes (e.g., 3 out of 10), causing the pinger to concentrate all transaction load on those 3 nodes while the other 7 (70%) receive no traffic and eventually fail health checks?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a value that causes integer overflow in the Hiero SDK's HbarFromTinybar() call, producing a negative or zero transfer amount that consensus nodes must validate and reject, generating malformed transaction load that degrades node performance?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoints containing extremely long domain names (e.g., 65535 characters), causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce an oversized address string that the SDK client cannot resolve, effectively removing those nodes from the network map and causing >=30% of nodes to be unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL that intermittently returns different node lists on each request, causing the pinger to be restarted (e.g., by a liveness probe failure) and each restart to receive a different subset of nodes, creating a rotating exclusion pattern that ensures different 30%+ subsets of nodes are starved of traffic over time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to '0.0.0' (a non-existent account), causing all transactions submitted by the pinger to be rejected by consensus nodes with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID, generating a stream of failed transactions that consume node validation resources and degrade performance for >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ms' and deploy multiple pinger instances simultaneously, each targeting a different subset of consensus nodes via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST, creating a coordinated transaction flood that overwhelms >=30% of nodes without any single instance appearing to be a brute-force attack?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint serve a nodesEnvelope where all serviceEndpoints have valid domain names that resolve to a single IP address (DNS round-robin to one node), causing all pinger transactions to be routed to that one node, overloading it while the remaining nodes (>=30%) receive no traffic?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a syntactically valid but cryptographically invalid key string that passes PrivateKeyFromString() but produces invalid signatures, causing every transaction submitted by the pinger to fail signature verification on consensus nodes, generating a stream of INVALID_SIGNATURE errors that consume node CPU for >=30% of nodes?
